name: apartment-tenement
summary: A single-room tenement apartment — a bed, a stove, a table, everything a family owns squeezed into four walls. Smells of damp and cabbage.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: small rectangle, often deeper than wide

primary_palette.note: family of four in one room — beds share a wall, table between
kitchen-corner dominates — everything orbits the stove
young couple with first child — bed, cradle, one trunk
the table is the center — eating, sewing, schoolwork all happen here

story_prompts: occupied — the mother mends by the stove, children already asleep in the bed
the rent is unpaid — the landlord's note on the table, half the possessions already sold
a death — the body laid on the table, the room dressed as dignified as poverty allows
a hideout — the usual family is out, a stranger squats in their room
overcrowded — one room, three beds, seven people, clothes washing on a line overhead

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: low (0.2-0.3) — one tallow candle and what little comes through the window

anti_patterns: no luxury of ANY kind — no carpet, no mirror, no painting. One prized possession at most.
every surface is shared — beds double for seats, tables for beds, nothing single-purpose
clutter is the texture — hung clothes, stacked crates, overlapping uses
damp, cold, tight — let the prose and lighting reinforce this
